- **IsSFlat** used `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`'s module-stalk instance (applied explicitly, since TC synthesis won't unfold `ringCatSheaf.obj`).

## What I built

The objective was `internalHom (I : X.Modules) : X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules`. After thorough scouting I confirmed this is a **genuine multi-iteration from-scratch Mathlib-gap layer** (no `internalHom`/`ihom`/`sheafHom`/`MonoidalClosed`/`Linear` for `Presheaf`/`SheafOfModules`, no over-open restriction functor — all loogle/grep-confirmed). Rather than fabricate the functor, I built its **foundational atom** axiom-clean — the per-section module structure that every internal-hom section carries:

- **`homModuleOverGlobalSections`** (Basic.lean:1093) — `Module Γ(Y,⊤) (M ⟶ N)` for `M N : Y.Modules`. A global function `r` acts on `φ` by scaling each section `φ.val.app U` by `r|_U`.
- **`linearOverGlobalSections`** (~1129) — `Linear Γ(Y,⊤) Y.Modules` (the whole module category is linear over global functions).
- 6 private helpers (`globalSectionRestrict` + ring-hom lemmas, `restrictScalars_map_smul`, `internalHomSmulVal`).

**Key technical crack:** the structure sheaf is valued in `RingCat`, which hides commutativity, so `ModuleCat`'s `Linear`-over-`CommRing` structure on hom-sets isn't directly available. Recovered the per-open `CommRing` via `inferInstanceAs (CommRing Γ(Y, U.unop))` (defeq to the `RingCat` carrier). Naturality of the scalar action proved via a semilinear-restriction lemma + `Linear.{comp,smul}_comp` + `φ.val.naturality`.
